description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray coloration and subtle white marbling throughout its surface. The rock has a somewhat rounded yet asymmetrical form, with one side appearing slightly flatter and the other more bulbous. Its surface texture is visibly rough and uneven, with natural indentations, minor cracks, and a matte finish that suggests a hard, weathered stone. The coloration is mostly a cool gray, interspersed with lighter patches and faint streaks, giving it a natural, unpolished appearance. There are no visible signs of artificial modification, markings, or integration with other materials. This rock would blend seamlessly into urban parks, landscaped areas, or as a natural obstacle in pedestrian zones.
description: A medium, irregular gray rock with rough, matte texture and subtle white marbling. Its surface is uneven and natural, with no visible markings or modifications.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest, flattest side with visible white marbling and a gently sloping surface.
description_view_1: Here, the rock appears more rounded and compact, with the roughest and most bulbous surface visible.
description_view_2: This side highlights the elongated profile of the rock, with a smoother, slightly sloped surface and more pronounced marbling.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a triangular, uneven aspect with visible cracks and a mix of flat and rounded surfaces.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
